Add WebSocket subscription server with binary protocol

- WireProtocol: UMDF-inspired binary framing (4-byte header: u16 length + u16 type)
  Message types for subscribe/unsubscribe, book snapshots, order events, trades, market data
- ClientSession: per-connection state with bounded Channel outbound queue (DropOldest)
- SubscriptionManager: lock-free architecture
  - Feed thread processes subscription requests via ConcurrentQueue (zero contention)
  - Dedicated snapshot thread reads books via seqlock (optimistic read + version check)
  - Incremental events forwarded to subscribed clients on feed thread
- WebSocketHost: Kestrel WebSocket server on configurable port
- SymbolRegistry: bidirectional FrozenDictionary for symbol<->securityId mapping
- Composite handlers: CompositeBookEventHandler, CompositeMarketDataEventHandler
- OrderBook seqlock: BeginWrite/EndWrite with Interlocked.Increment (~4ns overhead)
- ConsoleApp: --ws-port <port> flag to enable WebSocket server alongside replay

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add SyncStateFromEOV to replenish queue on exhaustion

When the playback queue is exhausted (e.g. radio/My Wave ending), send a
SyncStateFromEOV message to ask the Ynison backend to refresh the queue
from the centralized EOV service. This is the standard protocol mechanism
for queue replenishment — no public Ynison client has implemented it before.

Changes:
- ynison_client: add sync_state_from_eov() method
- provider: call EOV sync after completion signal in _advance_queue()
- tests: verify EOV sync is called on queue exhaustion
- ROADMAP: add queue replenishment strategies (proactive progress, rotor API)
- CHANGELOG: document SyncStateFromEOV feature

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): use in-script GITHUB_ENV override for reliability

The step-level env override (env: GITHUB_ENV: /dev/null) is not
reliably preventing MTP test executables from writing to the
runner's internal GITHUB_ENV file. Move the override to an
explicit export inside the run script, which directly modifies
the shell's environment and is inherited by all child processes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Make project production-ready with PostgreSQL

Replace Redis with PostgreSQL as the primary data store. Use a partial
unique index on (movie_id, seat_id) with INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
to atomically prevent double-booking.

Changes:
- Add config loader (DATABASE_URL, SERVER_PORT, HOLD_TTL env vars)
- Add graceful shutdown with signal handling
- Add pgx/pgxpool PostgreSQL adapter and connection pool
- Add SQL migration runner with schema_migrations tracking
- Create movies and bookings tables with partial unique index
- Implement PostgresStore (Book, ListBookings, Confirm, Release)
- Add background hold-expiry worker (cleans stale holds every 30s)
- Move movies from hardcoded slice to database (internal/movie package)
- Remove Redis store, memory store, concurrent store, and go-redis dep
- Fix BookingStore interface (add context.Context, error returns)
- Add domain error types (ErrSeatTaken, ErrSessionNotFound, etc.)
- Add JSON error responses with correct HTTP status codes
- Add input validation on all mutation endpoints
- Add GET /healthz endpoint (pings PostgreSQL)
- Add request logging, panic recovery, and CORS middleware
- Add handler unit tests with mock store (7 tests)
- Add PG integration tests with concurrent booking race test
- Add multi-stage Dockerfile and PostgreSQL docker-compose
- Add .env.example and .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(sf2): F7 multi-song support — up to 16 songs per .sf2 file

Multi-song architecture matching original SID Factory II:
- Shared sequence pool across all songs (memory efficient)
- Per-song order lists parsed from C64 memory layout
- Parser reads auxiliary data at 0x0FFB for song count + names
- Order lists for song N at: base + (N * orderListSize * trackCount)

Store actions:
- selectSong(idx): saves current order lists, loads new song's
- addSong(): creates new song with default order lists (max 16)
- removeSong(idx): removes song, adjusts indices
- renameSong(idx, name): rename up to 256 chars

Keyboard:
- F7: Songs dialog — switch/add/remove/rename songs via prompt
  Enter number to switch, '+' to add, '-N' to remove, '=N name' to rename

UI:
- DOM toolbar shows 'Song X/N' + song name when multi-song
- GL toolbar shows song indicator in info line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

add prompt_strategy config field, and fix webhook tests that leaked background
tasks causing 20s timeouts.

- discussion_pipeline.py: DiscussionPipeline + DiscussionContext
- coding_pipeline.py: CodingPipeline + CodingContext
- discussion_orchestrator.py: thin delegation (263 → 65 LOC)

commit.message:
feat(install-analyzers): recursive search for compiler DLL in compilerPath

Search subdirectories for Microsoft.Dynamics.Nav.CodeAnalysis.dll when
not found in the root of compilerPath. Fast path preserved: if the DLL
exists in the root directory, no recursive search is performed.

When multiple DLLs are found:
- All same TFM: succeed and log a table of paths and versions at INFO
- Different TFMs: fail with a descriptive error listing all conflicts

Improved error messages to distinguish missing directory from no DLL
found in directory tree.

Speed-based rendering: zoom enforcement, map freeze at 60x, live day counter

- Fix setSpeed zoom enforcement (was referencing 'Render' instead of 'Renderer')
- At 4x speed: auto-zoom to 1.0x if zoomed out further
- At 16x+ speed: auto-zoom to 1.5x minimum
- At 1x speed: free zoom to any level (was incorrectly clamped to 1.0)
- At 60x speed: freeze map rendering entirely (static cached frame)
- Add UI.updateDateDisplay() — lightweight day/year-only update
- Day/year display updates every frame even when map is frozen or render is skipped
- Use _loopFrameCount for UI throttle (always increments even when map frozen)

commit.message:
Reorganize release pipeline scripts and align documentation (#121)

### Why

The `scripts/` directory was a flat collection of confusingly-named CI
scripts (`ci-changesets.ts`, `ci-release.ts`, `ci-release-finalize.ts`,
`build-facet-cli.ts`, etc.) with no grouping to indicate which scripts
belong to which pipeline. The naming made it difficult to understand the
release architecture — even we had to reverse-engineer our own pipeline
during a planning session.

Additionally, several docs still referenced the old staging→promote
publishing strategy that was reverted in #80, and the `.circleci/`
source directories used unintuitive names (`src/` and `release-src/`).

### Details

**Scripts reorganization** — Moved from a flat layout to two
pipeline-scoped directories:
- `scripts/release/` — library package pipeline (`version.ts`, `tag.ts`,
`publish.ts`)
- `scripts/release-cli/` — CLI binary pipeline (`build.ts`,
`publish-platform.ts`, `publish-cli-package.ts`, `finalize.ts`,
`verify.ts`, `seed.ts`, `targets.ts`)

**IO adapter split** — Broke the 145-line god object (`lib/io.ts`) into
domain files (`lib/io/npm.ts`, `git.ts`, `github.ts`, `shell.ts`,
`console.ts`). Same flat interface via `lib/io/index.ts` — existing
`spyOn(io, 'method')` mocking patterns still work.

**Private package guard** — `release/publish.ts` now skips `npm publish`
for packages with `"private": true`. This prevents accidental publish
attempts when new internal packages (like upcoming platform adapter
packages) get version tags.

**Naming standardization** — Renamed `MAIN_PACKAGE_NAME` →
`CLI_PACKAGE_NAME` and `publish-main-package.ts` →
`publish-cli-package.ts` to avoid confusion between "main" (the branch)
and "main" (the CLI wrapper package).

**CircleCI source directories** — Renamed `.circleci/src/` →
`.circleci/development/` and `.circleci/release-src/` →
`.circleci/release/`, with the packed output now at `development.yml`
instead of `config.yml`.

**Documentation alignment** — Updated `ci-architecture.mdx`,
`release-pipeline.mdx`, `publishing.mdx`, and the distribution OpenSpec
spec to reflect the current direct-to-latest publishing strategy (no
staging tag, no promotion step). Removed dead `parsePublishedPackages`
code. Added READMEs with pipeline flow diagrams to `scripts/`,
`scripts/release/`, and `scripts/release-cli/`.

### Verification

`bun check` passes (all 13 turbo tasks). `bun run ci:check-pack`
confirms packed CI configs are in sync.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Medium risk because it changes CircleCI job commands and
release/publish scripts that drive tagging and npm publishing; mistakes
could break automation or publish/skip the wrong packages.
> 
> **Overview**
> Reorganizes the release automation into two explicit pipelines
(`scripts/release/` for library packages and `scripts/release-cli/` for
the CLI), updates CircleCI configs to call the new entrypoints, and
renames the packed CI config to `.circleci/development.yml`.
> 
> Refactors the scripts I/O “god object” into domain modules under
`scripts/lib/io/` while keeping the same flattened `io` interface, and
removes unused publish-output parsing code.
> 
> Tightens release behavior by adding a *private package guard* in
`release/publish.ts` (skip `npm publish` when `pkg.private`),
standardizes naming (`MAIN_PACKAGE_NAME` → `CLI_PACKAGE_NAME`), and
updates docs/specs to reflect direct-to-`latest` publishing (no
staging/promote) and the revised pipeline structure.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit
ab8f26af138a6158acdffb01b6ba1ef5b3faf844. Configure
[here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add MoCA test (Montreal Cognitive Assessment)

Frontend:
- MocaForm.tsx: full 7-domain form (visuoespacial, denominación,
  atención, lenguaje, abstracción, recuerdo diferido, orientación)
  with toggles for binary items, 0-3 selector for resta serial,
  live running total with color coding, clinical instructions per item
- TestFormDispatcher: register MoCA form
- RegisterSession: add MoCA category 'Evaluación cognitiva global · Cribado'
- EvaluationSetup: add MoCA to ALL_TEST_TYPES

Backend:
- raw_score_extractor: MoCA → total_bruto field
- calculator: _calculate_moca() — education correction (+1 if ≤12y),
  adjusted score as puntuacion_escalar, clinical classification
  (Normal ≥26 / DCL ≥18 / Moderado ≥10 / Grave <10)
  per Nasreddine et al. (2005)

feat: add Product Owner agent with rubber-duck multi-model reviews

Adds .github/agents/product-owner.agent.md — a benevolent PO agent that:
- Carries a mission statement for the repo
- Assesses codebase health, test coverage, UX, performance and tech debt
- Proposes up to three improvement candidates across multiple categories
- Stress-tests proposals using rubber-duck reviews from three model families
  (claude-opus-4.6, gpt-5.4, claude-haiku-4.5) in parallel
- Presents refined options with trade-off analysis for user to choose from
- Researches the chosen option and produces a concrete implementation plan

* feat: add layered input moderation with judge pattern

- Add checkForPromptInjection() detecting 7 injection patterns via regex
- Add moderateInput() calling omni-moderation-latest, returns all flagged categories
- Add ALWAYS_BLOCK_CATEGORIES set (sexual/minors, self-harm/instructions) that bypass judge
- Add judgeForMoviePlatform() using gpt-5.4-mini + zodResponseFormat structured output to approve/reject moderation flags in movie-platform context
- Wire Step 0 moderation into POST /api/movie-recommendation: injection check → moderation → always-block → judge
- Fix max_tokens → max_completion_tokens for gpt-5.4-mini compatibility
- Fix MovieService.getMovieByTitle to return undefined on HTTP failure instead of re-throwing; downgrade both log sites from error to warn
- Add full test coverage: 20 moderation unit tests + 5 route integration tests (25 total passing)

* feat: scale similarity display, markdown descriptions, calibration tool, and UI polish

- Scale similarity % to ceiling (0.62 = 100%) via scaleSimilarity() in utils/ui
- Add SIMILARITY_CEILING constant; update SimilarityBadge, MainMovieCard, SmallSuggestionCard
- Add MarkdownText component supporting *italic* and **bold** for AI descriptions
- Render AI descriptions with MarkdownText in MainMovieCard and ExpandedSuggestion
- Hide 'NR' age rating across all card components (TMDB results have no certification)
- Add duration to ExpandedSuggestion card with clock icon
- Fix JIT dedup: build localKeys from all local DB results, not just high-quality ones
  (prevents previously-seeded movies from re-appearing as TMDB results)
- Add scripts/calibrate-similarity.ts with built-in queries, auto-suggested threshold,
  and npm run calibrate-similarity command
- Add threshold calibration docs and scoring interpretation table to SERVICES.md

* feat: bulletproof TMDB search, localized fallback, single-use more-picks, test coverage

- getMovieById: direct TMDB ID lookup (no title ambiguity)
- getMovieByTitle: 4-tier cascade (exact → normalized → prefix → TMDB rank),
  two-pass (with year then without), ±1 year tolerance
- getMovieInfo: accepts optional tmdbId; uses direct lookup for TMDB-sourced movies
- localizedOverview: TMDB plot used as free AI description fallback (no extra API call)
- Language prompts: CRITICAL prefix + per-message reinforcement
- more-tmdb-picks route: batched AI descriptions, rate-limit logging, locale fallback
- Results page: single-use TMDB button (noMorePicks after any fetch attempt)
- Loading page: quiz data kept alive for more-picks; cleanup moved to reset buttons
- MovieService.test.ts: 16 tests covering all new and existing methods
- MovieService.mocks.ts: makeMockMovie factory, disambiguation and subtitle fixtures

* fix translation

* Run type-check

commit.message:
CRITICAL FIX: Enable multi-account auto-trading on all 3 accounts

- Fixed evaluate_symbol to use symbol_key for account+symbol tracking
- Extract actual symbol from config before fetching rates
- Use symbol_key for last_brick_state to track per account
- Each account now has its own Renko engine and brick color state
- Trades will now execute on ALL 3 accounts, not just the first

BEFORE: Only account 101510620 got trades
AFTER: All 3 accounts (101510620, 336245996, 1301212568) get trades
